text:
WHY DID BANK NIFTY CRASH TODAY⁉️

✅Reason is RBI’s new ECL norms (as discussed in the video)

👉India's 10-Year Bond Yields were over 9% a decade ago
👉Fast forward to 2018-19, it dropped to ~7.5%
👉And today it’s at ~6.5% (benchmark these days is 6%)

This fall in Bond Yields means a drop in risk premiums - it signals a structurally growing economy

📉And this decline in Bond Yields is not going unnoticed 

#StockMarket #BankNifty 

🧵🧵🧵

text:
UAE Golden Window, thank you Iran

Emirates NBD is essentially going to act as a market bellwether, testing whether international investor appetite has returned and if pricing has stabilized enough for other Gulf entities to follow suit.

In short: It’s a routine move for the bank, but a high-stakes "test" for the current recovery of the broader Middle Eastern financial markets.

🔥 Will keep eye wide open as many institutional investors are still hesitant, with UAE index names seeing some selling pressure as recently as this week

✅ if it oversubscribes heavily, it’s a sign that the market has already "priced in" the war risks and is ready to rotate back into Gulf credit.

✅✅ I'm bullish on the gulf and in 6 months
If that bond settles with 5x or 10x demand, I will ignite a stampede by triplicating the number of bulls.
html:
UAE Golden Window, thank you Iran

Emirates NBD is essentially going to act as a market bellwether, testing whether international investor appetite has returned and if pricing has stabilized enough for other Gulf entities to follow suit.

In short: It’s a routine move for the bank, but a high-stakes "test" for the current recovery of the broader Middle Eastern financial markets.

🔥 Will keep eye wide open as many institutional investors are still hesitant, with UAE index names seeing some selling pressure as recently as this week

✅ if it oversubscribes heavily, it’s a sign that the market has already "priced in" the war risks and is ready to rotate back into Gulf credit.

✅✅ I'm bullish on the gulf and in 6 months
If that bond settles with 5x or 10x demand, I will ignite a stampede by triplicating the number of bulls.
text_1:
UAE Golden Window, thank you Iran

Emirates NBD is essentially going to act as a market bellwether, testing whether international investor appetite has returned and if pricing has stabilized enough for other Gulf entities to follow suit.

In short: It’s a routine move for the bank, but a high-stakes "test" for the current recovery of the broader Middle Eastern financial markets.

🔥 Will keep eye wide open as many institutional investors are still hesitant, with UAE index names seeing some selling pressure as recently as this week

✅ if it oversubscribes heavily, it’s a sign that the market has already "priced in" the war risks and is ready to rotate back into Gulf credit.

✅✅ I'm bullish on the gulf and in 6 months
If that bond settles with 5x or 10x demand, I will ignite a stampede by triplicating the number of bulls.

text: La constance de certains hommes politiques est surprenante ! https://t.co/65vsWEBB0p
html:
Emirates NBD dollar bond sale kicks off as Gulf markets test recovery.

Dubai’s Emirates NBD is preparing a new US dollar bond sale, marking a potential turning point for Gulf debt markets after weeks of disruption.

The bank has launched investor meetings for a benchmark additional tier 1 (AT1) issuance, with a syndicate including Abu Dhabi Commercial Bank, Barclays, Citi, Emirates NBD Capital, First Abu Dhabi Bank, HSBC and JPMorgan appointed to arrange the deal.

This is the first public debt deal from the Gulf since the Iran war began. Markets have been largely paused during the conflict, with investors holding back.

The planned bond is a perpetual non-call six-year (PNC6) AT1 instrument — a type of security banks use to strengthen their capital base. These bonds typically offer higher returns to investors but also carry more risk.

Emirates NBD is entering the market from a position of strength. It holds investment-grade ratings from Moody’s and Fitch Ratings, and is the UAE’s second-largest bank by assets.

The lender has also been active in capital markets this year, issuing a Dhs1bn digital bond and a €500m green bond earlier in 2026.

Investor meetings, which began on April 27, will determine demand and pricing for the bond.

The market will be closely watching this development as it could open the door for other Gulf issuers to return — helping restore momentum to financing across the region.

text:
Looking back at Animenia Abu Dhabi, it was a celebration of creativity, passion, and the small businesses that bring it all to life. At Emirates NBD, supporting small businesses isn't a one-time moment. It's our ongoing commitment, today and always.

لم يكن معرض أنيمينيا أبوظبي مجرد فعالية، بل مساحة تحتفي بالإبداع، وتجمع الشغف بالمشاريع الصغيرة التي تمنحه الحياة. في بنك الإمارات دبي الوطني، لا يُنظر إلى دعم هذه المشاريع كحدث عابر، بل مسار مستمر نواكبه في كل يوم، ونواصل البناء عليه على المدى الطويل.

Very generous IPT ... expecting this to tighten to 6.25%

★★ EMIRATES NBD – US$750MM (WNG) REGS ONLY PERP NC6 AT1 BOND OFFERING – IPTS at 6.750% area ★★

NOT FOR RELEASE, PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION IN WHOLE OR IN PART TO THE UNITED STATES OR TO ANY U.S. PERSON (AS DEFINED IN REGULATION S UNDER THE SECURITIES ACT (AS DEFINED BELOW)), OR IN ANY OTHER JURISDICTION WHERE IT WOULD BE UNLAWFUL TO DO SO.

Issuer                                    Emirates NBD Bank PJSC (the “Issuer”)
Securities                                Perpetual Additional Tier 1 Capital Securities (the "Capital Securities")
Issuer Rating                             A1 (stable) by Moody’s / A+ (stable) by Fitch
Issue Rating                              Unrated
Ranking                                   The payment obligations of the Issuer under the Capital Securities will: (a) constitute Additional Tier 1 Capital of the Issuer; (b) constitute direct, unsecured, conditional and subordinated obligations of the Issuer that rank pari passu and without preference or priority amongst themselves; (c) rank subordinate and junior to all Senior Obligations; (d) rank pari passu with all Pari Passu Obligations; and (e) rank in priority only to all Junior Obligations. To the extent the Solvency Conditions are not satisfied or if a bankruptcy order in respect of the Issuer has been issued by a court in the UAE, claims shall be extinguished
Issue Size and Currency                   US$750mm (Will Not Grow)
Maturity                                  Perpetual Non-Call 6 years
IPTs                                      6.750% area
Pricing Date                              [28] April 2026
Issue Date/ Settlement Date               [5] May 2026
First Call Date                           [5] November 2031
First Reset Date                          [5] May 2032
Call Dates                                The First Call Date and any date thereafter up to and including the First Reset Date and any Interest Payment Date thereafter
Reset Dates                               The First Reset Date and every sixth anniversary thereafter
Interest Payment Dates                    [5] May and [5] November in every year, commencing on [5] November 2026
Interest Payment Amount                   Subject to interest cancellation, [ ] % p.a., payable semi-annually in arrear on each Interest Payment Date for the period from and including the Issue Date until but excluding the First Reset Date, thereafter reset on the First Reset Date and every sixth anniversary thereafter to a new fixed rate based on the 6-year US Treasury Rate on the relevant U.S. Securities Determination Date plus the Margin
Reset Margin                              [ - ] bps
Dividend Stopper                          Restriction of distributions on, redemption, acquisition or repurchase of share capital, Other Common Equity Tier 1 Instruments and pari passu/junior ranking securities, until the following Interest Payment Amount has been paid in full (or set aside)
Interest Payment Cancellation             Non-cumulative mandatory cancellation upon (i) there being insufficient Distributable Items to pay the interest amount and other distributions payable on the same date; (ii) breach of Applicable Regulatory Capital Requirements; (iii) Regulator’s requirement; or (iv) breach of Solvency Conditions; and optional cancellation at the Issuer’s election, including if the Issuer incurs a net loss during the relevant Interest Period (in each case subject to a dividend stopper)
Early Redemption                          At the option of the Issuer on any Call Date (at par); following a Tax Event (may occur at any time) (at par); and following a Capital Event (may occur at any time) (at 101% if redeemed prior to the First Call Date and at 100% if redeemed on or after the First Call Date), subject to conditions to redemption
Variation                                 Upon a Capital Event or a Tax Event, provided that the Capital Securities become or remain, as the case may be, Qualifying Tier 1 Instruments and subject to certain conditions including the variation not being materially less favourable to investors.
Non-Viability Loss Absorption (NVLA)      Full or, at the regulator’s sole discretion, partial write-down upon a Non-Viability Event
Solvency Conditions                       The Issuer’s payment obligations are conditional upon the following: (i) the Issuer being Solvent at all times during the relevant period; and (ii) the Issuer being able to make all payments in respect of the relevant Obligations, Senior Obligations and any outstanding Pari Passu Obligations and still be Solvent and (iii) the total share capital of the Issuer being greater than zero at all times during the relevant period
Day Count Fraction                        30/360
Denominations                             USD 200,000 with increments of USD 1,000 in excess thereafter
Issuer LEI                                54930029BCN8HF3B1286
Governing Law                             English Law
Listing                                   Euronext Dublin and Nasdaq Dubai
Joint Lead Managers and Joint Bookrunners Abu Dhabi Commercial Bank, Barclays, Citi, Emirates NBD Capital, First Abu Dhabi Bank, HSBC and J.P. Morgan
Billing and Delivery                      HSBC
ISIN / Common Code                        XS3357228504 / 335722850
Timing                                    Books open, Today’s Business
Stabilisation                             FCA/ICMA
Format                                    Regulation S, Registered, Category 2

text:
IFC = International Finance Corporation (World Bank Group). 
ADB = Asian Development Bank. 
NBD = Emirates NBD (UAE-based bank, full name Emirates NBD Bank PJSC). 

Foreign involvement: Emirates NBD (Dubai) is acquiring ~60%+ controlling stake (~$3B primary + open offer) with RBI nod April 2026—largest FDI in Indian banking. Legacy DFIs (IFC, ADB, British International Investment) hold low single-digit %. FPIs ~20-28% (global funds, no single dominant name). 

Board & Indian side: Professional—Chairman Chandan Sinha (ex-RBI), MD/CEO R Subramaniakumar. No promoter holding; top owners are domestic mutual funds/DIIs (~35-40%). No prominent political or specific corporate family ties in latest filings or board per public records. 

Details on https://t.co/MOjEBFfSH8.

No prominent Indian political figures or direct political connections on board or in major ownership per public records. Board is led by pros like Chairman Chandan Sinha (ex-RBI). 

Details in their latest shareholding filings on rbl.bank.
